question: A regular hexagon can be divided into six equilateral triangles. If the perimeter of one of the triangles is 21 inches, what is the perimeter, in inches, of the regular hexagon?

output.text:
The regular hexagon can be divided into six equilateral triangles, and since all equilateral triangles have the same side length, the side length of the hexagon is equal to the side length of the equilateral triangles. ки
Given that the perimeter of one of these equilateral triangles is 21 inches, we can find the side length of the hexagon by dividing the perimeter of the triangle by 3, because an equilateral triangle has three equal sides. ки
\[
\text{Side length of the hexagon} = \frac{\text{Perimeter of one triangle}}{3} = \frac{21}{3} = 7 \text{ inches}
\] ки
Since the hexagon is made up of six equilateral triangles, the perimeter of the hexagon is six times the side length of one triangle. ки
\[
\text{Perimeter of the hexagon} = 6 \times \text{Side length of one triangle} = 6 \times 7 = 42 \text{ inches}
\] ки
Therefore, the perimeter of the regular hexagon is $\boxed{42}$ inches. ки
